ksa_text: Knowledge of AI system transparency and disclosure requirements, including interaction notification obligations, synthetic content identification, deepfake disclosure, AI-generated content labeling, and the design of transparency mechanisms appropriate to different regulatory contexts.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AG-K-011 (AI Governance/Knowledge) excluded as out_of_scope (composite 0.378, highest: NIST AI RMF at 0.378). Domain prior: out. Core hits: 0. OOS hits: 0. Seniority: appropriate. Master data management: MDM platforms, golden records, entity resolution, data matching/merging, reference data management, and master data governance.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Exclude consistent with out_of_scope classification, composite 0.378, and AI Governance (AG) handling.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence challenge the exclusion of this AI Governance KSA for Master Data Manager?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: AI Governance competencies do not appear as Master Data Manager requirements in job postings. They are allocated to specialist roles. Exclusion consistent with market patterns.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Exclusion upheld by market evidence. AI Governance is not a Master Data Manager core competency. Final decision: exclude.
